era_context: Second Intermediate Period in Egypt; Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia; mature Indus Valley Civilization; Hittite Old Kingdom; early Shang dynasty in China (traditional dating).

events.title: Samsu-iluna's continued rule in Babylon
events.description: Samsu-iluna's reign continued. Cuneiform documents record ongoing administrative and legal activities. The Old Babylonian state maintained its institutions, though the dynasty's power was gradually eroding relative to rising Kassite influence in the region.
events.certainty_note: Attested in cuneiform sources; absolute dating uncertain.

historiographic_note: This year, like -1837 and -1836, is not marked by any specific documented event. The historical record for this period consists primarily of administrative continuity. The absence of year-by-year narrative for this era reflects the nature of surviving sources—administrative documents and archaeological stratigraphy do not typically provide precise annual events.
